---
# LOLBin proxy execution (rundll32/regsvr32/mshta)

**Dataset**: `xdr_data`

Living-off-the-land binaries proxy malicious code through trusted Windows tools. Filter PROCESS events to the classic LOLBins (`rundll32.exe`, `regsvr32.exe`, `mshta.exe`, `bitsadmin.exe`, `installutil.exe`) and group by binary with `values(causality_actor_process_image_name) as parents` to see WHO launched them — a LOLBin spawned by Office, a browser, or a script host is the T1218 abuse pattern (vs. a legitimate system parent). Pivot to `actor_process_command_line` on hits to inspect the proxied payload (a URL, a scriptlet, a DLL export).

```sql
dataset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and actor_process_image_name in ("rundll32.exe", "regsvr32.exe", "mshta.exe", "bitsadmin.exe", "installutil.exe")
| comp count() as execs, values(causality_actor_process_image_name) as parents by actor_process_image_name
| sort desc execs
```

_Query syntax live-verified on a real XSIAM tenant (narrow 30-min window). Adapt thresholds + the `contains`/`in` sets to your environment; filter early to keep the scan cheap._
